prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "平板转体" (plank with rotation) exercise, front-three-quarter view from the open (rotated-toward) side. Person posture: Athletic person at the top phase of a plank rotation — currently in a SIDE-PLANK-LIKE position after rotating 90 degrees from a high plank. The RIGHT HAND remains on the ground supporting the entire body weight, right arm fully extended. The LEFT ARM is FULLY EXTENDED STRAIGHT UP toward the ceiling, perpendicular to the body, fingers pointing up. The body is rotated 90 degrees so the left side of the torso faces the ceiling. The legs are STACKED on top of each other (left foot on top of right foot), only the right foot's outer edge contacts the ground. The body forms one straight line from feet to head. Head looks up toward the raised left hand. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL: ONE arm is on the ground, the OTHER arm is extended straight up toward the ceiling — NOT both hands on the ground.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: obliques (rotational core) and rectus abdominis. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: shoulder stabilizers and gluteus medius. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腹斜肌" pointing to the obliques (rotational core); "腹直肌" pointing to the rectus abdominis; "肩稳定肌 / 臀中肌" pointing to the shoulder and gluteus medius stabilizers. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
